 {"id":"bd-7l9.3","title":"Update AGENTS.md for FrankenMermaid project","description":"Replace the DCG-specific content in AGENTS.md with FrankenMermaid-specific guidance while preserving universal rules (no file deletion, git branch rules, Rust toolchain, code editing discipline, backwards compat stance, compiler checks, beads workflow, session protocol). Add: project overview (extracting mermaid engine from FrankenTUI into standalone lib+CLI+WASM), crate structure map, architecture diagram, key files table, testing strategy, the source-of-truth reference at /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid*.rs, and notes about the legacy_mermaid_code/ reference directory (mermaid-js, for format reference only, NOT a port target).

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:19.316238082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:35:09.071153151Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:35:09.071127944Z","close_reason":"Replaced DCG-specific AGENTS content with FrankenMermaid-specific guidance, crate architecture, source-of-truth references, testing strategy, CI details, and preserved universal safety/workflow rules.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-7l9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-7l9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7xyz","title":"Update README FNX section to honestly reflect current integration status","description":"## Problem
README Section "Optional FNX Integration" describes FNX (franken_networkx) as if it's a simple optional feature flag you can toggle on. In reality:
- There are ~25 open P1 beads under the bd-ml2r epic covering architecture, adapter, diagnostics, cycle scoring, ordering enrichment, telemetry, runtime budgets, UX controls, comprehensive verification, and rollout.
- Only 2 of those 25 beads are in-progress (bd-ml2r.1 and bd-ml2r.1.1 — the very first sub-tasks).
- The feature flags exist in Cargo.toml but the actual integration code is not yet written.

This is the project's most ambitious unfinished initiative, and the README makes it sound like a toggle.

## Fix
Update the README FNX section to:
1. Keep the existing Cargo feature flag documentation (it's accurate for the BUILD story).
2. Add a clear status note: "FNX integration is currently in early development. The feature flags compile but the integration surface is not yet functional. See the project's issue tracker for the bd-ml2r epic tracking this work."
3. Optionally add a brief roadmap: Phase 1 (undirected graph intelligence + diagnostics) → Phase 2 (directed graph analysis + ordering enrichment).

## Why This Matters
Understating the gap between "feature flag exists" and "feature works" is a soft form of README dishonesty. Users or contributors who enable `--features fnx-integration` and find it does nothing will lose trust. Honest disclosure costs nothing and builds credibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README FNX section includes honest status disclosure.
- No false impression that enabling the feature flag gives you working functionality.
- Roadmap or link to issue tracker for follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:48.482538455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:27:44.517402065Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:27:44.517103149Z","close_reason":"Added honest status disclosure (early development, feature flags compile but not functional), roadmap (Phase 1/2), and link to FNX_INTEGRATION.md ADR.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7xyz","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.465716866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-8im7","title":"Replace hardcoded cluster divider color with theme cluster_stroke","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded cluster divider stroke color (#64748b) with theme.colors.cluster_stroke.

## Why
State diagram cluster dividers (dashed lines separating composite state regions) use a hardcoded slate gray. This should match the cluster border styling of the active theme.

## Specific Changes
- fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs line ~1910: Change `.stroke("#64748b")` to `.stroke(&theme.colors.cluster_stroke)`

## Status
DONE (latest session). Verify committed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cluster dividers match cluster border color on all themes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:19.921075743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:02.671306825Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:02.670827508Z","close_reason":"Cluster divider now uses theme.colors.cluster_stroke","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-90m7","title":"ER: Implement relationship label rendering and key type highlighting","description":"# ER Diagram Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Relationship labels (the verb phrase between entities) are parsed but not always rendered prominently
- PK/FK/UK key type highlighting in attribute lists: should use bold/color/icon
- Entity attribute comments (quoted strings after attribute) not rendered
- Cardinality symbols at line endpoints need proper crow's foot rendering

## Implementation Plan
1. Ensure relationship labels render centered on the edge path
2. Add visual distinction for key types (PK = key icon or bold, FK = italic, UK = underline)
3. Render attribute comments in lighter/smaller text
4. Verify crow's foot cardinality symbols are correct for all 14 operator variants","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.640543165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T19:28:46.455826250Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T19:28:46.455573482Z","close_reason":"Added ER entity attribute rendering in SVG: compartmented box with entity name header, divider line, and attribute list with PK/FK/UK bold highlighting and key prefix labels. 2 new parser tests for attribute key types and comments. Golden snapshots updated. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["er","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-90m7","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-90m7","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Two entities with ||--o{ relationship and label → verify label renders centered on edge, crow's foot (o{) renders on target end
2. Entity with PK/FK/UK attributes → verify PK renders with key icon or bold, FK italic, UK underline
3. Entity with 'string email UK "Employee email"' → verify comment text renders below attribute
4. All 14 cardinality operators → verify correct endpoint symbols for each
5. Self-referencing entity (EMPLOYEE ||--o{ EMPLOYEE : manages) → verify self-loop with label
6. Entity with no attributes → verify empty entity box renders correctly","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-91wx","title":"Add theme-aware visual regression test suite","description":"## What
Create a test suite that renders each golden fixture diagram with every theme preset and verifies no theme produces illegible output.

## Why
The hardcoded color fixes are necessary but not sufficient. We need ongoing assurance that:
1. New code does not introduce new hardcoded colors
2. All 10 theme presets produce readable output
3. Theme changes in the future do not break diagram readability

## Proposed Approach
1. For each golden fixture (flowchart_simple, sequence_basic, state_basic, gantt_basic, pie_basic, class_basic, etc.):
   - Render SVG with each of the 10 theme presets
   - Verify the SVG is non-empty and well-formed
   - Verify no NaN/Infinity values in coordinates
   - Optionally verify contrast ratio between text colors and background (WCAG check)
2. Store as a new test in fm-cli/tests/ or fm-render-svg tests
3. Run as part of `cargo test --workspace`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test covers all 10 themes × all golden fixtures
- Test fails if any combination produces empty or malformed SVG
- Provides a safety net against future hardcoded color regressions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:52.895843408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:01.587764822Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:01.587247102Z","close_reason":"Added all_theme_presets_produce_valid_svg test: renders 6 diagram types × 10 theme presets, verifies non-empty SVG without NaN. Provides ongoing guard against theme-blind hardcoded colors.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-91wx","depends_on_id":"bd-1m4x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:38.535155416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-91wx","depends_on_id":"bd-5slz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:37.641776881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-91wx","depends_on_id":"bd-8im7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:36.718612946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-91wx","depends_on_id":"bd-i138","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:35.843205028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-91wx","depends_on_id":"bd-tl00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:34.912695609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-91wx","depends_on_id":"bd-u4c1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:33.990796828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
